name: Mondoñedo and O Cadramón Peak Loop
name_original: Mondonedo – vuelta por Monte O Cadramón
description: This difficult 32.3-mile mountain biking route near Mondoñedo offers challenging terrain, stunning views, and historic landmarks.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Mondoñedo offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain in Galicia, Spain. The region features a network of mountain bike trails that traverse river valleys, such as the Ouro and Tronceda, and ascend to hills like Monte de Lebre and Monte O Cadramón. Riders can expect a mix of natural beauty and historic landmarks, including ancient monasteries and cathedrals, integrated into the routes. The area provides options for different skill levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails with significant elevation gain. Best mountain bike trails in Mondoñedo The most popular mountain bike trail is Foz – vuelta por Monte de Lebre sendero del río Ouro, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route explores the Monte de Lebre and follows the Ouro river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mondoñedo and O Cadramón Peak Loop, a difficult 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and views from Monte O Cadramón. Local mountain bikers also love the Tunnel with holes – Iron bridge loop from A Pontenova, a 15.6 miles (25.1 km) trail leading through varied Galician scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Mondoñedo is defined by diverse landscapes, river valleys, and historic landmarks. The extensive network of mountain bike trails offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Mondoñedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mondoñedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo offers a diverse network of mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate paths suitable for those with some experience to more difficult routes demanding high fitness and technical skill. The trails traverse varied landscapes, including river valleys like the Ouro and Tronceda, and ascend to hills such as Monte de Lebre and Monte O Cadramón.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with significant elevation gain in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, Mondoñedo features several mountain bike routes with substantial elevation gain. For a challenging ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993674597/mondonedo-and-o-cadramon-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo and O Cadramón Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 32.3 miles (52.0 km) and includes over 1500 meters of ascent, offering panoramic views from Monte O Cadramón. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24569418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Vilaoudriz loop from As Rodrigas&lt;/a&gt;, with nearly 1400 meters of elevation gain over 46.7 km.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Mondoñedo?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Mondoñedo integrate stunning natural features. You can explore river valleys like the Ouro and Tronceda, which are known for their waterfalls. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto do Coro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tronceda River Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are notable highlights. The region also boasts wild, jungle-like nature, providing cool riding even in summer.

Q: Are there any historic landmarks accessible by mountain bike in Mondoñedo?
A: Absolutely. Mondoñedo's mountain biking routes often intertwine with significant historical and cultural sites. Riders can encounter historic landmarks such as the Monastery of San Salvador and the Mondoñedo Cathedral. The Fonte Vella (Old Fountain) and the Vilaoudriz kilns, the largest set of calcination kilns in the northwest of the peninsula, are also points of interest that can be explored during your rides.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Mondoñedo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994341338/foz-vuelta-por-monte-de-lebre-sendero-del-rio-ouro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz – vuelta por Monte de Lebre sendero del río Ouro&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 15.5-mile (25.0 km) loop exploring Monte de Lebre and following the Ouro river. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993674597/mondonedo-and-o-cadramon-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo and O Cadramón Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Mondoñedo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Mondoñedo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, challenging sections, and the integration of natural beauty with historic landmarks.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, Mondoñedo offers a good selection of moderate trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24611002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel with holes – Iron bridge loop from A Pontenova&lt;/a&gt; is a 15.6-mile (25.1 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 8 minutes to complete, offering varied Galician scenery. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altar Beach – Beach of the Cathedrals loop from Foz&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.4 km route with moderate elevation.

Q: Is Mondoñedo a good destination for mountain biking with a family?
A: Mondoñedo offers a range of trails, and while some are quite challenging, there are options that families with older children or those seeking less strenuous rides might enjoy. Look for routes described as 'moderate' with lower elevation gains and shorter distances. Always check the specific trail details on komoot to ensure it matches your family's skill level and endurance.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo's diverse landscapes make it suitable for mountain biking across different seasons. The region's 'wild nature' can offer cool riding even in summer, providing a pleasant escape from the heat. Spring and autumn generally offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, while winter rides are possible, though conditions may vary. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for mountain biking in Mondoñedo?
A: Based on available information, there are no specific permits mentioned for general mountain biking on the trails around Mondoñedo. However, it's always good practice to respect local signage, private property, and environmental regulations. If you plan to ride through protected areas, it's advisable to check with local authorities for any specific rules.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Mondoñedo?
A: While many outdoor areas in Galicia are dog-friendly, specific rules can vary by trail or protected area. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them. Always check individual trail descriptions or local regulations for any specific restrictions regarding dogs.

Q: Where can I find parking near the mountain bike trails in Mondoñedo?
A: Parking availability can vary depending on the specific trailhead. Many routes start from towns or villages like Mondoñedo or Foz, where public parking is typically available. For routes starting in more remote areas, look for designated parking spots or consult the route details on komoot, as users often share information about parking locations in their tour descriptions or comments.
tips: The Cathedral Basilica of the Virgin of the Assumption of Mondoñedo is one of the episcopal sees of the diocese of Mondoñedo-Ferrol in Galicia. The temple, in the Romanesque style with later additions and reforms, also has contributions from the Baroque and Gothic styles. Although its construction dates from the 13th century, the cathedral was not completed until the 14th century. Later, during the Middle Ages, a choir, the ambulatory and the sacristy were built, while the cloister with a square plan with semicircular arches and Doric columns dates from the 17th century.
Mountain peak part of the Xistral massif nature reserve at 1056m above sea level the massif is now connected to the wind power grid
Adventure area with many trails along the river. Points of interest such as the aquadact and the waterfalls of the Tronceda River

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Mondonedo – vuelta por Monte O Cadramón route is classified as difficult. It demands a very good level of fitness and advanced riding skills due to its significant distance (52 km) and substantial elevation changes (around 1,500 meters up and down). Riders should be prepared for more than 5 hours of riding, and some sections may require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this challenging mountain bike route is varied and technical. You can expect a mix of natural singletrack, gravel paths, unpaved sections, and some asphalt or paved roads, especially on climbs to gain significant altitude. The technical difficulty ranges from S3 to S6, indicating challenging surfaces that require advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers several notable points of interest. You'll start and end in the historic town of Mondoñedo, home to the impressive Mondoñedo Cathedral. The trail also takes you to Pico O Cadramón, offering extraordinary views of the Monte Xistral mountain range. Additionally, you can discover the scenic Tronceda River Waterfalls.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point of the Mondonedo – vuelta por Monte O Cadramón route is conveniently located in Mondoñedo, right next to a parking lot. This makes it easy to begin your adventure without worrying about finding a spot for your vehicle.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route passes through the ZEC Serra do Xistral. While there are generally no specific entrance fees for recreational activities like mountain biking in this protected area, it's always advisable to check local regulations for any specific permits required for certain activities or group sizes, especially if you plan to deviate from established trails. Respecting the natural environment and any local guidelines is crucial.

faqs.question: Is the Mondonedo – vuelta por Monte O Cadramón trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in Galicia are dog-friendly, given the challenging nature and length of this specific mountain biking route, it might not be ideal for all dogs. If you do bring your dog, ensure they are fit enough for a long and strenuous ride, and always keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas like ZEC Serra do Xistral, to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety. Remember to carry enough water for both you and your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike the Mondonedo – vuelta por Monte O Cadramón route is typically during the spring and autumn months. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more stable, offering pleasant temperatures for strenuous activity. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures, rain, or even snow at higher elevations, making the already challenging terrain more difficult and potentially hazardous.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the "Enlace do Camiño do Norte co Camiño Inglés" and the "Northern Route (Galiza)". It also briefly touches upon "[CIMA LU19] O Cadramón", "Camiño natural de San Rosendo (Foz - Mondoñedo)", and "Camiño da Infesta".

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in Mondoñedo, a historically rich town and one of Galicia's "Most Beautiful Pueblos", you'll find various facilities. Mondoñedo offers cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, making it a convenient base for your mountain biking adventure and a great place to relax after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring essential mountain biking gear, including a well-maintained mountain bike, helmet, gloves, and appropriate riding attire. Additionally, carry plenty of water and snacks, a first-aid kit, navigation tools (like a GPS device or the Komoot app with offline maps), a repair kit for your bike, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions, especially at higher altitudes.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, the Mondonedo – vuelta por Monte O Cadramón route is not suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers. It is rated as 'difficult' and requires advanced riding skills and very good fitness. The significant distance, elevation changes, and technical terrain (S3-S6) make it a challenging undertaking best reserved for experienced riders.
